title: Jaundice documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.
assessment.rationale: The exact liver-history section supports jaundice, pruritus, abdominal pain, weight loss, medicine, travel, exposure, comorbidity, and alcohol context without assigning a cause.

source.title: Guidelines on the management of abnormal liver blood tests
source.population: Children and adults with abnormal liver blood tests, with recommendation-specific adult and paediatric differences.
source.setting: Primary and secondary care assessment of abnormal liver blood tests.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected result-context, history, adult liver-aetiology-screen, and clinician-referral documentation. The BSG resource page was last reviewed in 2021 with review due in 2025; UAE pathways and patient-specific interpretation require clinician review.

exact_location.heading: Response to abnormal liver blood tests — clinical history

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports age, hepatitis-risk context, jaundice, abdominal pain, weight loss, pruritus, comorbidity, prescribed or non-prescribed drug history, travel or exposure context, and alcohol history.

assessment.text: Supports clinician recognition of dysphagia, weight-loss combinations, and jaundice in recommendation-specific age and symptom contexts; it does not assert cancer or an automatic referral decision.
assessment.rationale: The exact upper-GI section supports clinician-assessed jaundice and concern context with age qualifiers without diagnosing cancer or assigning urgency.

source.title: Suspected cancer: recognition and referral
source.population: Children, young people, and adults with symptoms or signs that may indicate cancer, subject to recommendation-specific age criteria.
source.setting: Primary care recognition, safety netting, investigation, and referral.
source.exclusions: Exact for selected suspicious skin-lesion, safety-netting, and age- or risk-qualified respiratory recommendations; international suspected-cancer pathways require UAE clinician review.
